stories.title: Durham County Commissioners Advance $968M FY2026 Budget — Here's What's Inside
stories.excerpt: The Board of Commissioners voted 4–1 to advance the proposed budget, which includes a 3.2-cent property tax increase, $45M for affordable housing, and the largest public school capital outlay in county history.

stories.title: Durham Public Schools Requests $400M+ County Allocation in FY2027 Budget Ask
stories.excerpt: Durham Public Schools, which serves over 33,000 students, is requesting the largest education budget ask in county history — prioritizing teacher pay, mental health staff, and capital repairs.

stories.title: County Affordable Housing Bond: Where Are We Two Years In?
stories.excerpt: A status report on Durham County's $95M affordable housing bond shows 1,200 units funded, but advocates say the county must move faster as rents continue to rise.

stories.title: Durham County Expands Crisis Intervention Team Countywide
stories.excerpt: The county's co-responder mental health program, which pairs clinicians with law enforcement, will expand to all shifts in 2026 following a positive two-year pilot evaluation.

stories.title: Ellerbe Creek Restoration Gets $2M State Grant
stories.excerpt: Durham County Stormwater Services will restore 1.4 miles of degraded habitat in northeast Durham using the NC DEQ grant, benefiting both flood mitigation and wildlife.

stories.title: GoTriangle Proposes Durham-Chapel Hill BRT Expansion
stories.excerpt: Regional transit planners presented an expanded bus rapid transit corridor to county commissioners, projecting 18,000 daily riders by 2030 if funded in the next transportation bond.

stories.title: Commissioners Adopt New Equity in Contracting Policy
stories.excerpt: The Board unanimously approved a new supplier diversity policy requiring county procurement to track and improve participation by minority- and women-owned businesses.

stories.title: DPS Names Three Finalists in National Superintendent Search
stories.excerpt: The Durham Public Schools Board of Education revealed the three candidates under consideration to lead the district's 56 campuses after a months-long national search.
